<summary>Agent "Validate AC 1-6 time logging" completed</summary>
<result>Now I'll compile my findings. Based on my comprehensive analysis of the codebase, here's the validation report:

---

## PRD Acceptance Criteria Validation Report

### AC-1: Employees can log time daily or weekly
**Status: PASS**

**Implemented:**
- Backend supports per-date entry logging via `timesheetEntries` table with `date` field (schema/timesheet-entries.ts:24)
- Frontend displays 7-day week grid with individual date columns (TimesheetGrid.tsx:53-58)
- Employees can enter hours for each day independently via EntryCell component
- TimesheetGrid shows daily totals across all charge codes (lines 62-70)
- Time entry page auto-saves every 30 seconds, allowing incremental daily logging

**Notes:**
- The system is fundamentally week-based (periodStart/periodEnd define the submission period)
- Employees enter time on a daily granularity but submit weekly timesheets
- Both daily and weekly workflows are supported within the weekly period model

---

### AC-2: System enforces timesheet cutoff every 15th and end of month
**Status: PASS**

**Implemented:**
- Backend scheduler (timesheets.scheduler.ts:21-34) runs cron jobs:
  - Mid-month: `@Cron('5 0 15 * *')` — locks timesheets on the 15th at 00:05
  - End-of-month: `@Cron('5 0 28-31 * *')` — locks timesheets on the last day of month
- Cutoff logic (lockTimesheetsBefore, lines 36-83):
  - Locks `cc_approved` and `manager_approved` timesheets whose periodEnd ≤ cutoff date
  - Force-locks `submitted` (unapproved) timesheets past the cutoff
- Submission cutoff in service (timesheets.service.ts:238-250):
  - 3-calendar-day grace period after period end (cutoffDate = periodEnd + 3 days)
  - Returns 403 ForbiddenException if now > cutoffDate
- Frontend displays cutoff warnings (time-entry/page.tsx:329-342):
  - Shows banner when ≤2 days remaining
  - Shows "submission closed" message when past cutoff
  - Disables Submit button when closed

**Notes:**
- Cutoff enforcement has two layers: the scheduler locks approved timesheets on 15th/EOM, and submission validation prevents new submissions after grace period
- Notifications sent to users with draft timesheets (sendCutoffWarnings, lines 85-122)

---

### AC-3: System requires minimum total 8 hours per day before timesheet submission
**Status: PASS**

**Implemented:**
- Backend validation (timesheets.service.ts:268-336):
  - `validateMinimumHours()` called during submit (line 253)
  - Checks each weekday (skips weekends/holidays from calendar) for ≥8 hours total across all charge codes
  - Only validates days that have passed (≤ today), skips future dates
  - Throws BadRequestException with details array listing each short day
- Frontend validation (time-entry/page.tsx:225-249):
  - `checkMinHoursAndSubmit()` calculates daily totals before submission
  - Shows dialog with incomplete days (hours logged / 8h required)
  - Must explicitly confirm before submitting
- Backend and frontend validation independently enforce the same 8-hour minimum

**Notes:**
- Minimum is per weekday (Mon-Fri), not including weekends
- Validation accounts for non-working days from the `calendar` table (holidays)
- Both backend and frontend validate to prevent invalid submissions

---

### AC-4: Employees can allocate hours to one or more charge codes
**Status: PASS**

**Implemented:**
- Data model supports multiple entries per timesheet (timesheetEntries table has no unique constraint on timesheetId+date, allowing multiple rows per date)
- Frontend TimesheetGrid displays multiple rows (one per charge code), each with daily cells
- Employees can add charge codes via ChargeCodeSelector component (time-entry/page.tsx:466-470)
- Backend upsertEntries (timesheets.service.ts:147-221):
  - Accepts array of entries with date + charge_code_id + hours
  - Validates charge codes are assigned to user (lines 164-184)
  - Filters and inserts all non-zero hour entries
- Frontend grid calculates daily totals summing across all charge codes (TimesheetGrid.tsx:62-70)

**Notes:**
- The system stores one entry per (timesheet, chargeCodeId, date) — multiple charge codes can have hours on same day
- No limit on number of charge codes per timesheet

---

### AC-5: Employees can split time across multiple registered tasks in one day
**Status: PASS**

**Implemented:**
- Grid structure inherently supports this: each row is a charge code, each cell is (chargeCodeId, date)
- Multiple charge code rows can have hours entered for the same date
- TimesheetGrid.tsx calculates daily totals by summing all charge codes for a given date (lines 62-70)
- Backend stores each (chargeCodeId, date, hours) as a separate entry in timesheetEntries
- Example: Employee can log 4h on PRG-001 and 4h on PRG-002 on Monday, totaling 8h

**Notes:**
- Data model and UI design explicitly support splitting hours across tasks/charge codes
- Daily total row in grid shows the sum of all charge codes for that day

---

### AC-6: Employees can add optional work descriptions
**Status: PASS**

**Implemented:**
- Database schema (timesheet-entries.ts:26):
  - `description: text('description')` — nullable field for optional descriptions
- Frontend EntryCell (EntryCell.tsx:93-112):
  - Yellow corner triangle indicator when description exists
  - Note button appears on hover when no description
  - Click opens EntryNoteDialog
- EntryNoteDialog (EntryNoteDialog.tsx):
  - Modal for editing note text (textarea, 4 rows)
  - Display format: "Note for [chargeCodeName] · [date]"
  - Save/cancel buttons
- Time-entry page (page.tsx:286-298):
  - handleDescriptionChange updates descriptions state
  - Descriptions saved alongside hours in upsertEntries call (line 183)
- Backend upsertEntries (timesheets.service.ts:202):
  - `description: e.description || null` — stored if provided

**Notes:**
- Descriptions are optional per entry (charge code + date combination)
- UI provides clear affordance for adding notes via corner triangle and hover button
- Descriptions are displayed in modal dialog, not inline in grid

---

## Summary Table

| AC | Status | Key Implementation | Notes |
|---|--------|-------------------|-------|
| AC-1 | PASS | Daily per-date entry UI + weekly submission model | Supports both daily logging and weekly submission |
| AC-2 | PASS | Cron scheduler (15th, EOM) + 3-day grace period submission cutoff | Locks timesheets + prevents submissions after cutoff |
| AC-3 | PASS | validateMinimumHours() on backend + frontend dialog | Validates ≥8h per weekday, skips weekends/holidays/future dates |
| AC-4 | PASS | Multiple charge code rows in grid + upsertEntries batch API | Employees add codes via selector, validate assignment |
| AC-5 | PASS | Grid structure + daily total calculation | Multiple charge codes per day automatically sum |
| AC-6 | PASS | Optional description field + EntryNoteDialog UI | Descriptions saved per (chargeCodeId, date) entry |

**Overall: All 6 acceptance criteria are FULLY IMPLEMENTED in the codebase.**</result>

## PRD Acceptance Criteria Validation Report

### AC-7: Charge codes support hierarchy levels: Program, Project, Activity Type, Task

**Status: PASS**

**Implementation:**
- Backend schema (`charge-codes.ts`): Four-level enum defined as `chargeCodeLevelEnum` with values `['program', 'project', 'activity', 'task']`
- Backend service enforces hierarchy rules via `LEVEL_HIERARCHY` map (lines 22-27 of service)
- Frontend UI displays all four levels with color-coded badges in ChargeCodeTree
- Frontend form supports creating charges at any level with parent validation

**Evidence:**
- `backend/src/database/schema/charge-codes.ts`: Lines 13-18 define the enum
- `backend/src/charge-codes/charge-codes.service.ts`: Lines 22-27 enforce hierarchy rules
- `frontend/src/app/(authenticated)/charge-codes/page.tsx`: Lines 47-52 display level badges
- `frontend/src/components/charge-codes/ChargeCodeForm.tsx`: Lines 56-61 define level options

---

### AC-8: System supports charge code creation at any hierarchy level, with mandatory support at top program level

**Status: PASS**

**Implementation:**
- Backend validation enforces that only program level can be created without a parent (lines 159-163 of service)
- All other levels require a parent at the correct hierarchy level
- Frontend form shows parent selector for project/activity/task, but not for program
- Backend endpoint `/charge-codes` (POST) is restricted to `admin` and `charge_manager` roles

**Evidence:**
- `backend/src/charge-codes/charge-codes.service.ts`: Lines 159-163 enforce parent requirement for non-programs
- `backend/src/charge-codes/charge-codes.controller.ts`: Line 45 restricts POST to `@Roles('admin', 'charge_manager')`
- `frontend/src/components/charge-codes/ChargeCodeForm.tsx`: Lines 239-262 conditionally show parent selector; line 107 `needsParent = !editData && level !== 'program'`

---

### AC-9: Charge code ID supports prefix/format for level differentiation and charge roll-up

**Status: PASS**

**Implementation:**
- Charge code IDs are auto-generated with level-specific prefixes (PRG, PRJ, ACT, TSK)
- IDs follow format: `PREFIX-NNN` (e.g., PRG-001, PRJ-042)
- Path field stores hierarchical path for roll-up (e.g., `PRG-001/PRJ-001/ACT-002/TSK-003`)
- Frontend displays charge code IDs and paths in detail view

**Evidence:**
- `backend/src/charge-codes/charge-codes.service.ts`: Lines 15-20 define prefixes; lines 283-296 implement auto-generation logic
- `backend/src/database/schema/charge-codes.ts`: Line 26 stores path field
- `frontend/src/app/(authenticated)/charge-codes/page.tsx`: Line 280 displays charge code ID in detail header

---

### AC-10: Charge code owner can configure and control access in hierarchy

**Status: PARTIAL**

**Implementation:**
- Backend enforces access control: only owner, approver, or admin can modify access (lines 221-232 of service)
- Frontend AccessManager component allows adding/removing users from assigned users list
- Charge code owner/approver can be set at creation time
- Update endpoint (`PUT /:id/access`) handles user assignment/removal

**Missing/Gaps:**
- No explicit hierarchical access control shown (e.g., parent owner cannot automatically control child charge codes)
- Access control appears to be flat per charge code (via `chargeCodeUsers` junction table) rather than hierarchical delegation
- No evidence of parent-child access inheritance or cascade rules

**Evidence:**
- `backend/src/charge-codes/charge-codes.service.ts`: Lines 218-257 implement access control via `updateAccess()` method
- `backend/src/database/schema/charge-code-users.ts`: Junction table stores user-to-charge-code relationships
- `frontend/src/components/charge-codes/AccessManager.tsx`: Full UI for managing assigned users
- `frontend/src/app/(authenticated)/charge-codes/page.tsx`: Lines 373-378 render AccessManager tab

---

### AC-11: Charge code stores required attributes: Charge Code ID, Project/Program, Cost Center, Activity Category, Budget Amount, Charge Code Owner, Charge Code Approver, Valid Date Range, Billable/Non-Billable flag

**Status: PASS**

**Implementation:**
All required attributes are persisted in the database schema and exposed in the API:

| Attribute | Field | Schema | Form | Display |
|-----------|-------|--------|------|---------|
| Charge Code ID | `id` | ✓ | Auto-generated | ✓ |
| Project/Program | `programName` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| Cost Center | `costCenter` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| Activity Category | `activityCategory` | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| Budget Amount | `budgetAmount` | ✓ (numeric) | ✓ | ✓ |
| Charge Code Owner | `ownerId` | ✓ (UUID FK) | ✓ | ✓ |
| Charge Code Approver | `approverId` | ✓ (UUID FK) | ✓ | ✓ |
| Valid From | `validFrom` | ✓ (date) | ✓ | ✓ |
| Valid To | `validTo` | ✓ (date) | ✓ | ✓ |
| Billable/Non-Billable | `isBillable` | ✓ (boolean) | ✓ | ✓ |

**Evidence:**
- `backend/src/database/schema/charge-codes.ts`: Lines 20-39 define all fields
- `backend/src/charge-codes/dto/create-charge-code.dto.ts`: Lines 20-83 validate all input fields
- `frontend/src/components/charge-codes/ChargeCodeForm.tsx`: Lines 200-394 render all form fields
- `frontend/src/app/(authenticated)/charge-codes/page.tsx`: Lines 324-366 display all attributes in overview tab

---

## Summary

| AC | Status | Notes |
|----|--------|-------|
| AC-7 | PASS | All four hierarchy levels fully implemented with validation |
| AC-8 | PASS | Creation at any level supported; program is mandatory top level |
| AC-9 | PASS | Prefix-based ID generation (PRG/PRJ/ACT/TSK) with path tracking |
| AC-10 | PARTIAL | Per-charge-code access control works; no hierarchical inheritance shown |
| AC-11 | PASS | All 10 required attributes stored, validated, and displayed |

**Overall: 4.5/5 PASS** — Implementation is comprehensive with all required attributes and hierarchy support. AC-10 is partially complete (works at charge code level but lacks explicit hierarchical delegation patterns).</result>

<summary>Agent "Validate AC 12-17 approval/reports" completed</summary>
<result>Now let me create a comprehensive validation report. Based on my thorough review of the codebase, here are my findings:

---

## PRD Acceptance Criteria Validation Report

### **AC-12: Timesheet approval flow supports Employee → Line Manager → Charge Code Approver**

**Status: PASS**

**What's Implemented:**
- ApprovalsService implements two-stage approval workflow:
  - Stage 1: Manager approval (Manager approves submitted timesheet → status: `manager_approved`)
  - Stage 2: Charge Code approver approval (CC owner/approver approves → status: `cc_approved` or `locked`)
- Service verifies approver authority:
  - Manager: checks `profiles.managerId` matches approver
  - CC Approver: checks `chargeCodes.ownerId` OR `chargeCodes.approverId` matches approver
- Auto-lock logic: if no CC approvers exist, timesheet auto-locks after manager approval
- Multi-approver support: tracks all required CC approvers and locks only when all approve
- ApprovalLogs table tracks all approval actions with timestamps and comments
- Frontend: Approvals page shows both manager queue and CC owner queue separately

---

### **AC-13: Approval functions support submit weekly timesheet, bulk approval, reject with comments, and lock approved timesheets**

**Status: PASS**

**What's Implemented:**
- `approve()` - individual approval with optional comment, auto-locks when all stages complete
- `bulkApprove()` - approves multiple timesheets with error handling per timesheet
- `reject()` - rejects with required comment, stores in `rejectionComment` field, creates approval log
- Timesheet submission via TimesheetsService (submit method)
- Auto-locking mechanism:
  - After final CC approval (status → `locked`)
  - Manual cutoff: TimesheetsScheduler locks timesheets on 15th and end-of-month
  - Force-locks submitted but unapproved timesheets at cutoff
- Frontend: ApprovalQueue component with approve/reject/bulk approve actions
- Comment history tracked in approvalLogs table

---

### **AC-14: System calculates actual cost as: Actual Cost = Logged Hours × Employee Cost Rate by Job Grade**

**Status: PASS**

**What's Implemented:**
- BudgetsService.recalculate():
  - Iterates all timesheet entries
  - Looks up user's `jobGrade` from profiles
  - Fetches matching CostRate for jobGrade with date range validation (`effectiveFrom`/`effectiveTo`)
  - Calculates: `calculatedCost = hours × hourlyRate`
  - Updates `timesheetEntries.calculatedCost` for each entry
- CostRatesService: CRUD for job grade rates with effective date ranges
- Cost rates applied per user per logging date (supports rate changes over time)
- Hierarchy rollup: costs aggregate from leaf charge codes → parents
- Frontend: Budget page displays actual spent based on calculated costs

---

### **AC-15: Reporting supports monitoring of actual vs planned charging, budget overrun forecast, overrun-driving tasks, team chargability vs target, and financial impact from over budget and low chargability**

**Status: PASS**

**What's Implemented:**

1. **Actual vs Planned Charging:**
   - `BudgetsService.getForecast()` calculates burn rate and forecast at completion
   - `ReportsService.getProjectCostReport()` shows budget vs actual vs variance

2. **Budget Overrun Forecast:**
   - `BudgetsService.recalculate()` calculates `forecastAtCompletion` using burn rate
   - Formula: `actualSpent + (burnRate × remainingDays)`
   - Stored in budgets table, retrieved in alerts and reports

3. **Overrun-Driving Tasks (Root Cause):**
   - `BudgetsService.findRootCauseActivity()` identifies highest-cost child charge code
   - BudgetAlert includes `rootCauseActivity` field showing which task drove overrun
   - ReportsService passes this through in `getBudgetAlerts()`

4. **Team Chargeability vs Target:**
   - `ReportsService.getChargeabilityReport()` calculates per-team rates
   - Target: 80% billable hours
   - Groups by root program, calculates billable/total hours ratio
   - Supports filtering by team

5. **Financial Impact:**
   - `ReportsService.getFinancialImpact()` calculates:
     - Over-budget cost (sum of overruns)
     - Low chargeability cost: `(targetChargeability - actualRate) × totalHours × avgCostRate`
     - Net impact: `overBudgetCost + lowChargeabilityCost`
   - Breaks down by team and charge code
   - Shows margin analysis for billable revenue vs cost

---

### **AC-16: System generates outputs: project cost reports, program financial tracking, resource utilization dashboards, activity distribution analytics, budget overrun alerts, and low chargeability alerts**

**Status: PASS**

**What's Implemented:**

1. **Project Cost Reports:**
   - `ReportsService.getProjectCostReport()` - hierarchical breakdown (2-3 levels)
   - Shows budget, actual, variance, percent used per level

2. **Program Financial Tracking:**
   - `ReportsService.getFinancialImpact()` - breaks down by root program (team)
   - Shows total budget, hours, costs, margin, margin% per program

3. **Resource Utilization Dashboards:**
   - `ReportsService.getUtilizationReport()` - per period, includes:
     - Working days calculation (from calendar, excluding weekends/holidays)
     - Per-employee logged vs available hours
     - Department-level aggregation
     - Utilization rates calculated

4. **Activity Distribution Analytics:**
   - `ReportsService.getActivityDistribution()` - hours by activity category
   - Shows percentage breakdown, sorted by volume

5. **Budget Overrun Alerts:**
   - `BudgetsService.getAlerts()` - returns alerts with severity (red/orange/yellow)
   - Thresholds: red >100%, orange >90%, yellow >80% or forecast > budget
   - Includes root cause activity
   - Logs scheduled daily at 8 AM (BudgetsScheduler)

6. **Low Chargeability Alerts:**
   - `BudgetsService.getChargeabilityAlerts()` - per-employee alerts
   - Severity: red <60%, orange <70%, yellow <80%
   - Calculates cost impact of chargeability gap
   - Frontend: AlertList component displays both types

---

### **AC-17: Advanced features support Teams-based logging/inquiry, reminder notifications, incomplete logging summary for Charge Manager, weekly insight summary to Program Owner and Cost Center Owner, project tracking sheet upload/link, and auto calendar for weekends/holidays/vacations**

**Status: PARTIAL**

**What's Implemented:**

1. **Teams-based logging/inquiry:**
   - ✅ TeamsBotService with natural language parsing
   - ✅ Commands: `log`/`logged`/`add` for time entry, `budget status` inquiry, `timesheet show`
   - ✅ `handleTimeLog()` - logs hours via Teams message
   - ✅ `handleBudgetInquiry()` - returns budget status
   - ✅ `handleTimesheetInquiry()` - shows current week timesheet
   - ✅ Integrated via IntegrationsController

2. **Reminder Notifications:**
   - ✅ `NotificationService` (scheduler) - daily 9 AM timesheet reminders
   - ✅ `IntegrationNotificationService.sendTimesheetReminders()` - incomplete logging reminders
   - ✅ `IntegrationNotificationService.sendApprovalReminders()` - for pending approvals
   - ✅ TimesheetsScheduler - cutoff warnings on 15th/month-end
   - ✅ Stored in notifications table

3. **Incomplete Logging Summary for Charge Manager:**
   - ⚠️ **PARTIAL** - Reminders exist but no dedicated "summary" endpoint
   - ✅ `IntegrationNotificationService.sendApprovalReminders()` notifies managers of pending timesheets
   - ✅ `IntegrationNotificationService.sendManagerSummary()` generates weekly status (not fully utilized in UI)
   - ❌ No dedicated admin view for charge manager to see team's incomplete entries

4. **Weekly Insight Summary to Program Owner and Cost Center Owner:**
   - ✅ `ReportsScheduler.handleWeeklyInsightSummary()` - runs Monday 7 AM
   - ✅ Aggregates hours and costs by program/cost center
   - ✅ Sends to PMO/Finance/Admin and charge code owners
   - ✅ Stored as notifications with summary data

5. **Project Tracking Sheet Upload/Link:**
   - ✅ `ProjectUploadService` - CSV upload for charge codes
   - ✅ Parses: project_name, charge_code, budget, start_date, end_date, owner_email
   - ✅ Upsert logic: creates or updates charge codes and budgets
   - ✅ Endpoint: IntegrationsController has upload endpoint
   - ✅ Error reporting per row

6. **Auto Calendar for Weekends/Holidays/Vacations:**
   - ✅ `CalendarService`:
     - `populateWeekends()` - auto-generates weekend entries for given year
     - `createHoliday()` - add/update holidays
     - `updateHoliday()` / `deleteHoliday()` - manage holidays
     - `createVacation()` / `getMyVacations()` / `approveVacation()` - vacation requests
     - Vacation approvals via manager
   - ✅ `getWorkingDays()` - counts working days excluding weekends, holidays, vacations
   - ✅ Frontend: Admin Calendar page for managing holidays
   - ✅ Used in utilization calculations and timesheet reminders
   - ✅ VacationRequests table tracks requests with approval workflow

## **Minor Gap Identified:**

**AC-17 Incomplete Logging Summary:**
- Notifications are generated for managers showing pending timesheet statuses
- However, there is no dedicated **"Incomplete Logging Summary" dashboard/endpoint** for charge managers to view their team's incomplete entries in one place
- Reminders exist but should ideally have a dedicated admin view showing all incomplete timesheets by employee/period

All other criteria are comprehensively implemented with both backend logic and frontend UI.</result>
